info.name: Microsoft Exchange Microsoft Graph Mailbox Import Export API

params.name: user-id
params.description: The unique identifier or user principal name

items.docs: Discover the mailboxes associated with a user, including primary mailbox and archive mailbox information. Returns mailbox metadata needed for import and export operations.

params.name: user-id
params.description: The unique identifier or user principal name

params.name: mailbox-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mailbox

items.docs: List all folders in the specified mailbox for content discovery. Returns folder hierarchy with metadata needed for targeted import and export operations.

params.name: user-id
params.description: The unique identifier or user principal name

params.name: mailbox-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mailbox

params.name: folder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the folder

params.name: $select
params.description: Comma-separated list of properties to include

items.docs: List items in a specific mailbox folder for export. Returns items with their metadata for batch export processing. Supports pagination for large folders.

params.name: user-id
params.description: The unique identifier or user principal name

params.name: mailbox-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mailbox

params.name: folder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the folder

params.name: item-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the item

items.docs: Export the full-fidelity content of a specific mailbox item in MIME format. Returns the complete item with all properties, headers, and attachments preserved for migration scenarios.

info.name: Microsoft Exchange Import item into folder

params.name: user-id
params.description: The unique identifier or user principal name

params.name: mailbox-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the mailbox

params.name: folder-id
params.description: The unique identifier of the folder

items.docs: Import a full-fidelity item into the specified mailbox folder. Accepts MIME content and creates the item preserving all properties and metadata. Used for mailbox migration and content copying scenarios.
